title: AT&T Business and Palo Alto Networks Partner to Deliver Quantum-Resilient SASE Fabric - Quantum Computing Report
gist: AT&T Business and Palo Alto Networks integrated Prisma SD-WAN with AT&T's fiber/cellular network into a Quantum-Resilient SASE Fabric using hybrid classical+PQC key exchanges (IETF RFC 9370/9242/8784), TPM-based hardware root of trust, and direct Service Provider Interconnect to defend distributed enterprise branches against 'harvest now, decrypt later' threats without added latency.

insights.claim: AT&T Business and Palo Alto Networks combined Prisma SD-WAN with AT&T's global fiber and cellular network into a native Quantum-Resilient SASE Fabric, building on AT&T's earlier Cisco-powered quantum-safe SD-WAN launch, to protect distributed enterprise perimeters against 'Harvest Now, Decrypt Later' threats without adding network latency or requiring hardware replacement at branch locations.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: AT&T is now running parallel quantum-safe network partnerships with both Cisco (SD-WAN) and Palo Alto Networks (SASE), indicating carriers are racing to bundle PQC into core connectivity products as a competitive differentiator ahead of the PQC mandate wave.
insights.quote: Building on its recent launch of a quantum-safe Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-WAN) powered by Cisco, AT&T Business has expanded its secure portfolio through a new collaboration with Palo Alto Networks.

insights.claim: The data plane uses hybrid key exchanges per IETF standards RFC 9370, 9242, and 8784, combining classical algorithms (for legacy compatibility) with quantum-resistant keys simultaneously, avoiding packet fragmentation.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Explicit adherence to named IETF hybrid key-exchange RFCs signals the industry is converging on standards-based hybrid PQC rollouts rather than proprietary schemes, reducing interoperability risk for enterprise buyers.
insights.quote: Adopting official Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF) standards—specifically RFC 9370, 9242, and 8784—the data plane utilizes hybrid key exchanges.

insights.claim: The fabric is built with 'crypto-agility by design' so that as NIST updates its post-quantum cryptography ciphersuite guidelines, edge systems can be updated automatically via the cloud without physical hardware overhauls.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Crypto-agility as a design principle reduces stranded-asset risk for enterprise buyers if NIST's PQC standard recommendations shift, a recurring theme across multiple vendors in this space (also seen with enQase).
insights.quote: As the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) continues to update its post-quantum cryptography (PQC) ciphersuite guidelines, edge systems can be updated automatically via the cloud without requiring physical hardware overhauls.

insights.claim: At the branch edge, Palo Alto Networks' Prisma SD-WAN ION appliances establish a hardware root of trust using Secure Boot and TPM 2.0 chips, verifying only cryptographically signed firmware/kernels/OS can execute, while dual classical+quantum-resistant credentials are issued to every connected device via Hybrid PKI.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Hardware-rooted trust (TPM 2.0 + Secure Boot) combined with hybrid PKI at distributed branch/retail/clinic edges addresses a commonly overlooked attack surface, relevant for assessing the depth of the PQC rollout beyond just core network links.
insights.quote: Featuring Secure Boot and integrated Trusted Platform Module (TPM 2.0) chips, these appliances verify that only cryptographically signed firmware, kernels, and operating systems can execute during startup.
